checks.description: After reset release, all output clocks become defined (no X/Z) within a few CLK_in cycles.
notes: Ensures basic post‑reset stability without assuming exact latency.
checks.pass_condition: Each of CLK_50, CLK_10, and CLK_1 is observed as 0 or 1 (no X/Z) within 5 CLK_in cycles after reset deassertion.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset transitions from asserted to deasserted.

checks.description: Verify that CLK_10 toggles at least once within a 12‑cycle observation window after reset release.
notes: Does not require exact cycle count; only a minimum progress guarantee.
checks.pass_condition: At least one rising or falling edge of CLK_10 is detected within 12 consecutive CLK_in cycles following reset release.
checks.trigger_condition: While observing the design after reset deassertion.

checks.description: Observe that CLK_10 does not assume X or Z values during the observation window after reset release.
notes: Conservative functional guardrail; does not assert exact toggle timing.
checks.pass_condition: All sampled values of CLK_10 are either 0 or 1 (no X/Z) for the duration of the window.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is deasserted and the design is observed for at least 12 CLK_in cycles.

checks.description: Verify that CLK_1 toggles at most once per 50 CLK_in cycles after reset release.
notes: Provides a conservative upper‑bound progress check without fixing exact latency.
checks.pass_condition: No more than one toggle of CLK_1 is observed within any 50‑cycle window of CLK_in after reset release.
checks.trigger_condition: During extended observation after reset deassertion.

checks.description: Observe that CLK_1 does not assume X or Z values during the observation window after reset release.
notes: Conservative functional guardrail; does not assert exact toggle timing.
checks.pass_condition: All sampled values of CLK_1 are either 0 or 1 (no X/Z) for the duration of the window.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is deasserted and the design is observed for at least 110 CLK_in cycles.

checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

notes: Stability-style property used instead of throughput-specific claims.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'RST' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
Protocol oracle strictness is downgraded because the contract or plan is weak.

unresolved_items: Golden interface described clock 'CLK_50', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
Golden interface described clock 'CLK_10', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
Golden interface described clock 'CLK_1', but the RTL port is not a scalar input-like signal.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: If the counter cnt_10 reaches a value of 4, CLK_10 is toggled by inverting its current value, and the counter cnt_10 is reset to 0.
Spec/reset hint could not be mapped to a known reset port: If the counter cnt_100 reaches a value of 49, CLK_1 is toggled by inverting its current value, and the counter cnt_100 is reset to 0.
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
Contract or plan confidence is limited; oracle generation is intentionally conservative and may omit value-level functional checks.
